skill.name: invalid-governance-skill
skill.description: A known-invalid fixture for testing skill marketplace governance validation.
skill.summary: A fixture for testing governance validation failures in skill marketplace pipelines.
skill.author: yaojingang

security_audit.summary: The two static findings are false positives after context review. SKILL.md contains only a short governance fixture description, with no cryptographic code, reconnaissance commands, prompt injection, or executable behavior. The skill is not malicious, but it is intentionally unsuitable for marketplace publication as a functional skill.

low_findings.title: Static finding dismissed: weak cryptographic algorithm
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged SKILL.md line 3, but that line is only the description text 'Test fixture for governance validation.' No weak algorithm, hashing operation, secret handling, or cryptographic implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged line contains plain descriptive metadata and no code. There is direct evidence that the finding is a keyword false positive.

low_findings.title: Static finding dismissed: system reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged SKILL.md line 6, but that line is only the heading '# Invalid Governance Skill.' No host enumeration, system command, network lookup, or environment inspection appears in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged line is a Markdown heading with no operational instruction. There is no evidence of reconnaissance behavior in the reviewed context.

dangerous_patterns.title: False positive: weak cryptographic algorithm pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The analyzer reported a weak cryptographic algorithm pattern at SKILL.md line 3. The actual line is descriptive frontmatter text about governance validation, not a cryptographic algorithm or implementation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Manual review confirms the line contains only prose metadata. No cryptographic operation is present.

dangerous_patterns.title: False positive: system reconnaissance pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The analyzer reported a system reconnaissance pattern at SKILL.md line 6. The actual line is a Markdown title naming the governance fixture, not a command or instruction to inspect a system.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Manual review confirms the line is a heading. No command execution, host inspection, or data collection behavior is present.

content.value_statement: Skill marketplaces need reliable checks that reject invalid submissions. This fixture provides a known governance failure case for testing validators and review workflows.
actual_capabilities: Provides a minimal skill fixture for governance validation tests
Documents that the fixture exists to verify governance failures
Includes metadata that can be compared with governance policy rules
Supports audit workflows for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code skill submissions
Helps reviewers confirm that invalid marketplace entries are rejected
limitations: Does not provide a production user workflow
Contains no executable automation or validation engine
Requires an external validator to produce pass or fail results
Should not be published as a functional marketplace skill

use_cases.title: Marketplace Policy Testing
use_cases.description: Use the fixture to confirm that marketplace governance checks reject invalid skill submissions.

use_cases.title: Audit Workflow Verification
use_cases.description: Use the fixture as a known failure case when testing security and content audit pipelines.

use_cases.title: Contributor Training
use_cases.description: Use the fixture to show contributors why governance metadata must match marketplace rules.

prompt_templates.title: Check Fixture Purpose
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this skill fixture and explain what governance behavior it is intended to test.

prompt_templates.title: Run Governance Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this fixture against our marketplace governance rules and summarize each expected failure.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Valid and Invalid Skills
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare this fixture with a valid skill and identify the governance differences that should affect publication.

prompt_templates.title: Design CI Guardrails
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a CI review plan that uses this invalid governance fixture to verify rejection behavior before release.

output_examples.input: Review the governance fixture for publication readiness.
output: The fixture is intentionally not publication ready.
It should be used to verify that governance checks reject invalid submissions.
No executable security threat was found in the reviewed skill text.
The weak cryptography alert is a false positive from descriptive text.
The reconnaissance alert is a false positive from the Markdown heading.
The skill still should not be published as a functional marketplace entry.

best_practices: Keep this fixture in test environments instead of user-facing marketplace catalogs.
Pair the fixture with a validator that reports the exact governance failures.
Document expected rejection behavior so audit results remain consistent.
anti_patterns: Do not treat this fixture as a template for production skills.
Do not publish it as a usable end-user skill.
Do not ignore governance failures because security findings are false positives.

faq.question: What is this skill for?
faq.answer: It is a test fixture for checking that marketplace governance validation rejects invalid skill submissions.

faq.question: Is this a functional skill?
faq.answer: No. It contains a short description and fixture metadata, but no production workflow or executable automation.

faq.question: Are the static security findings confirmed?
faq.answer: No. Both static findings are false positives after manual review of the referenced SKILL.md lines.

faq.question: Can it be published in a marketplace?
faq.answer: No. It is intentionally designed for governance failure testing and should remain outside production catalogs.

faq.question: Does it access files, networks, or environment variables?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The reviewed skill content does not include executable code or operational instructions.

faq.question: Who should use this fixture?
faq.answer: Platform engineers, security reviewers, and maintainers can use it to test governance review workflows.
